name: Double Chocolate Chunk Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, beat butter or margarine with an electric mixer on medium speed for about 30 seconds until softened.
2. Add sugar, cocoa, and baking powder; beat until combined.
3. Beat in the eggs.
4. Beat in as much of the flour as possible.
5. By hand, stir in any remaining flour, chopped white baking bar, and semisweet chocolate.
6. Divide dough into two portions.
7. Shape each portion into a 9-inch long log.
8. Place logs about 4 inches apart on a lightly greased cookie sheet.
9. Flatten logs slightly until about 2 inches wide.
10. Bake in a 375°F oven for 20 to 25 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted near the center comes out clean.
11. Cool on the cookie sheet and wire rack for 1 hour.
12. With a serrated knife, cut each log diagonally into 1/2 inch thick slices.
13. Lay slices, cut side down, on an ungreased baking sheet.
14. Bake slices in a 325°F oven for 8 minutes.
15. Turn slices over and bake for 7 to 9 more minutes, or until biscotti are dry and crisp (do not overbake).
16. Cool thoroughly on a wire rack.
17. Store the biscotti in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 1 week, or freeze in a freezer container for up to 6 months.
